summary: Scope is right-sized: 4 already-triaged defects (1 P2 mfe0 + 3 P3 hrmw/jaeg/k9a8) ride one spec because they share a theme (lifecycle CLI surprises callers) but no shared code path. In-Scope's 7 files match the verified targets exactly; no creep, and panel/ownership/doc-sync gates (099/100) plus unchanged --emit-only semantics are correctly Out-of-Scope. DECOMPOSITION into 4 beads is clean: R1 (next.go L213 claim path + select.go) vs R2 (NEW release.go + one additive root.go AddCommand line) touch different files, so R1+R2 are separable despite both living near next/claim+cmd/mindspec. NOTE (not blocking): R2 and R4 BOTH edit internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go but DIFFERENT functions — R4 edits InitSpecWorkspace (the CreateBranch(specBranch,"HEAD") at L86); R2 routes release worktree removal through the existing WorktreeOps.Remove interface (L38, already used at L252/348/356/403), likely adding a new release helper. No line overlap, but bead planning should sequence R2/R4 to avoid a textual merge conflict (like spec 100's plan.go). gitutil has NO default-branch/fetch helpers today, so R4 adds them fresh with zero conflict surface. DOGFOODING SAFETY verified on all three vectors: (a) R1's claim change cannot affect 101's own beads — they are claimed with the CURRENT installed binary, rebuilt only post-merge, so the change is not retroactive; (b) R4's branch-base change only touches spec-create — 101's worktree already exists (old HEAD behavior), and bead dispatch (DispatchBead, L116-118) branches off SpecBranch(specID) not origin/main, so it is untouched; future specs get a MANDATED graceful offline fallback (R4 + Design Decision: origin absent/fetch fails -> branch from local HEAD + emit WARN, a warn-and-continue NOT a hard failure), which covers offline CI; (c) R2+R4 both editing the executor cannot mid-spec self-break completes — completes use the INSTALLED binary (rebuilt only post-merge), so a bead rebuilding its local binary does not change what later beads' completes execute. ClaimBead's masking wrap (beads.go L165) and the absence of any release verb in root.go (L216-252) are both confirmed, matching R3/R2. APPROVE: no creep, no self-block footgun, and the only decomposition concern (R2+R4 executor co-edit) is a planning NOTE, not a CHANGES.
